text: reminder: <https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/display/FINERACT/FSIP-7%3A+traditional+merge+strategy|do not squash commits before merging PRs>. Local rebase by contributor is OK, rebasing on github is not. In short, if all commits are verified, we're doing this right. If not, we're missing signatures or we have invalidated them by inappropriately rewriting commits (e.g. squashing)

Hi everyone! I'm Deepak Kumar S S, a GSoC 2026 applicant for Apache Fineract.
I'm a backend Java/Spring Boot engineer with 4.9 years of experience, most recently
working on the ASX CHESS Replacement project handling millions of trade settlements daily in TCS.

I've submitted two GSoC proposals:
1. FINERACT-2439 ‚ÄĒ Self-Service BFF API (Large, 350hrs)
2. FINERACT-2485 ‚ÄĒ Transaction Idempotency for Savings and Loans (Medium, 175hrs)

Just raised my first contribution ‚ÄĒ migrating integration tests from RestAssured
to fineract-client-feign as part of FINERACT-2441:
<https://github.com/apache/fineract/pull/5658>

gsoc-fineract-evidence

Happy to be here and looking forward to contributing!

Hi everyone! :wave: I've raised my second contribution PR for FINERACT-2441 (fineract-client-feign migration for integration tests):
*PR:* <https://github.com/apache/fineract/pull/5659>
*What it does:*
Migrates `ClientExternalIdTest` from RestAssured to `fineract-client-feign`
‚ÄĘ Adds 7 new typed feign methods to `ClientHelper` for external-id operations (`addClientAsPerson`, `getClientByExternalId`, `updateClientByExternalId`, `deleteClientByExternalId`, `getClientAccounts`, `getProposedTransferDate`, `getObligeeData`)
‚ÄĘ Removes manual JSON payload construction in tests
Would appreciate any feedback or review! :pray:
This looks good <@U0AMVAW0Y6Q> but please make sure you are fully finishing the migration, so once the new methods are added to use fineract-client-feign, the old ones to be deleted...

Hi everyone, does <https://mifos.slack.com/archives/C028634A61L/p1773772117111409> make sense (react :+1:/:-1: please)? When you merge something on github, *do not rebase*. Per <https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/display/FINERACT/FSIP-7%3A+Traditional+Merge+Strategy|FSIP-7>, <https://github.com/apache/fineract/pull/5663|I'm going to modify .asf.yaml on Monday to enforce this>.

Hi everyone! :wave: I would love a quick set of eyes on my recent PR if anyone has bandwidth. I am prototyping some infrastructure for the `fineract-client-feign` migration.
*PR #5646:* <https://github.com/apache/fineract/pull/5646> *What it does:* Adds E2E test scenarios for SavingsAccount insufficient balance withdrawals using Feign directly (including `FeignException` handling).
*Question for the team:* There is a great point in the PR comments about why I tagged the new tests with `@Skip`. I followed the existing convention pending PS-1088, but I would love the community's guidance on whether the team prefers new E2E tests to be skipped or active right now!
Any feedback would be greatly appreciated. Thank you! :pray:

Hi All

I am running Apache Fineract 1.14. How can I enable customer self-service APIs such as:

Login ‚Üí /self/authentication
‚Üí View accounts ‚Üí /self/accounts
‚Üí Check loans ‚Üí /self/loans
‚Üí Transfer money ‚Üí /self/accounttransfers

Additionally, I would like to enable the following endpoints:

‚ÄĘ /self/*
‚ÄĘ /self/beneficiaries
‚ÄĘ /self/charges
‚ÄĘ /self/accounttransfers
‚ÄĘ /self/savingsaccounts
‚ÄĘ /self/savingsaccounts/{id}

‚ÄĘ /self/loans
‚ÄĘ /self/loans/{loanId}
‚ÄĘ /self/loans/{loanId}/transactions
Could you please guide me on how to enable and configure these for end customers? Thanks in advance

*Subject:* Issue with Self-Service Plugin Tables in Fineract 1.15

I am using the self-service plugin for user onboarding:
<https://github.com/openMF/selfservice-plugin>
I followed the below steps:
```#git clone <https://github.com/apache/fineract.git>
#cd /opt/fineract
# git branch
* develop
#./gradlew clean build -x test -x rat

# Verify build
#ls /opt/fineract/fineract-provider/build/libs/
fineract-provider-1.15.0-SNAPSHOT.jar```
Then, I built the self-service plugin:
```#git clone <https://github.com/openMF/selfservice-plugin.git>
#cd selfservice-plugin
#./mvnw -Dmaven.test.skip=true
#cp /opt/selfservice-plugin/target/selfservice-plugin-1.15.0-SNAPSHOT.jar /opt/fineract/libs/```
My systemd configuration is as follows:
```[Unit]
Description= CBS Application Service
After=network.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
WorkingDirectory=/opt/fineract/fineract-provider
ExecStart=/usr/bin/java -Duser.timezone=UTC \
  -Dloader.path=/opt/fineract/libs/ \
  -Dspring.config.additional-location=file:/opt/fineract/override.properties \
  -jar build/libs/fineract-provider-1.15.0-SNAPSHOT.jar
SuccessExitStatus=143
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=10
StandardOutput=append:/var/log/fineract/fineract.log
StandardError=append:/var/log/fineract/fineract-error.log

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target```
After startup, I can see the following self-service tables:
```SHOW TABLES LIKE '%m_selfservice%';

m_selfservice_beneficiaries_tpt
m_selfservice_user_client_mapping```
However, the following tables are missing:
‚ÄĘ `m_selfservice_user`
‚ÄĘ `m_selfservice_registration`
I have attached the self-service plugin logs for reference.
Could you please guide me on why these tables are not being created and how to resolve this issue?

Hi chat, what's the easiest way to add a new tenant to a fineract installation?

Well, there is only 1 one at the moment:
‚ÄĘ Add 2 entries
    ‚ó¶ 1 into the `tenant connections` table
    ‚ó¶ 1 into the `tenants` table
‚ÄĘ Restart backend
    ‚ó¶ It will initialized the new tenant

set the channel description: Mifos channel for discussion around one of our integrations Apache Fineract (R).  This channel is for getting Mifos Community support and collaboration.   The channel by Apache Fineract (R) is here <https://app.element.io/#/room/%23fineract:matrix.org>

Hi all! Please see the updated description. *This chat is now _unofficial_ for Apache Fineract*. This channel will remain the *official _Mifos-side chat_* for collaboration with the substantial <https://mifos.org|Mifos> community. For chatting about <https://fineract.apache.org/|Fineract>, please go to <https://matrix.to/#/#apache-fineract-home:matrix.org> . See also: <https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/FINERACT-2660|FINERACT-2660>. <https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=91554327#FAQ(frequentlyaskedquestions)-IsthisMifos?|Here's a FAQ> about the difference Mifos and Fineract.

Thank you <@U07ECHYJYD8> Mifos wishes the Apache Fineract (R) Community success with their new channel it is great to see the momentum and expertise that Apache Fineract has already within that community in such a short period of time.

We will of course continue to collaborate strongly as we do with all key integrations, and hopefully this channel will give a clearer space for the Mifos Community to discuss and innovate around that integration and support each other before bringing ideas to the Fineract Community.  It will also provide support within Mifos for this integration as we have similar channels for other integrations.
